title: Black Desert Resort Is Unlike Anything in Golf
excerpt: We played it before the hype hit. Here's the honest take on Utah's most talked-about new course.
author: Cameron
body:
## The First Tee

We pulled up to Black Desert Resort on a Tuesday morning and I'll be honest — the photos don't do it justice. The course sits against these massive black lava formations that look like they were dropped from another planet. It's the kind of setting that makes you check your phone just to make sure you're actually in Utah.

## The Course Itself

Designed by Tom Weiskopf, Black Desert plays to about 7,400 yards from the tips but the yardage is almost irrelevant. The wind off the desert rips through certain corridors, and the lava rock framing every hole turns routine shots into decisions.

**The standout holes:** 4, 9, 14, and 18. Hole 14 is as dramatic as anything I've seen in the US — a par 3 playing over a lava field with a green that falls away on three sides.

## Should You Play It?

If you're routing a Southwest trip and skipping Black Desert, you're making a mistake. Combine it with Entrada and you've got one of the best two-course days in American golf.

> *"The kind of course you call your buddy about from the parking lot."* — Cameron

## The Details

- **Location:** St. George, Utah
- **Green Fees:** $250–$350 depending on season
- **Best Time:** October–April (summers are brutal)
- **Book Via:** [Tee Times USA](https://teetimesusa.com)
